prompt:
You receive an argument from a debate on "Abortion".
Check whether it bundles multiple distinct claims into one sentence.
If yes, split it into atomic arguments (one claim each), preserving the stance.
If it already expresses a single claim, return it unchanged.

Important: Concessive fragments ("Although X", "While X", "Despite X") are rhetorical
hedges, NOT independent arguments. Drop them entirely — only keep the main claim.

Self-containment rule: Every output argument MUST be understandable without
any surrounding context. If a fragment contains pronouns like "it", "them",
"this", "they" without a clear referent, replace the pronoun with the explicit
noun from the original sentence. If the referent cannot be recovered, drop
the fragment entirely.

Argumentative content rule: Only keep claims that take a position or make
an evaluative assertion. Pure factual statements without evaluative content
(e.g. "X once spoke about Y") are not arguments — drop them.

Argument [pro29] (pro):
The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year , many of those in developing countries where safe and legal abortion services are difficult to access .

Respond ONLY with a JSON array, no markdown. Use IDs like "pro29", "pro29b", "pro29c", ...:
[
  {"id": "pro29",  "text": "...", "stance": "pro"},
  {"id": "pro29b", "text": "...", "stance": "pro"}
]

Argument [pro7] (pro):
" If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability , it may go so far as to pro-scribe abortion during that period , except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother . "

[

  {"id": "pro7b", "text": "...", "stance": "pro"}
]
raw_response: [{"id":"pro7","text":"If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.","stance":"pro"}]

parsed.text: If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.

Propose attack candidates for the following argument pairs from a debate on "Abortion".

An attack exists when one argument:
- asserts a conclusion logically incompatible with the other's conclusion — the one claim can be true only if the other is false (rebuttal), OR
- undermines a premise the other relies on (undermining), OR
- shows the other's conclusion does not follow even if its premise is true (undercutting)

Pair pro7:pro63
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[pro63] (pro) Not all abortions are unjustified.

Pair pro7:pro63b
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[pro63b] (pro) Abortion would be justified if the same justification could be applied to killing an adult human.

Pair pro7:pro58
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.

Pair pro7:pro36
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[pro36] (pro) The National Cancer Institute, the American Cancer Society, and the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists all refuted the claim that abortion can lead to a higher probability of developing breast cancer.

Pair pro7:pro27
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[pro27] (pro) They also ruled that the word "person" in the Constitution did not include a fetus.

Pair pro7:con140
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con140] (con) Susan B. Anthony and Elizabeth Cady Stanton spoke out against abortion.

Pair pro7:con23
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con23] (con) I will always support legislation that would end abortion or lead us in the direction of ending abortion.

Pair pro7:con9
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con9] (con) Abortion is not permissible from the point at which individual human identity is realized.

Pair pro63:pro58
  A=[pro63] (pro) Not all abortions are unjustified.
  B=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.

Pair pro63:pro36
  A=[pro63] (pro) Not all abortions are unjustified.
  B=[pro36] (pro) The National Cancer Institute, the American Cancer Society, and the American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists all refuted the claim that abortion can lead to a higher probability of developing breast cancer.

direction    = exactly one of: A_ATTACKS_B / B_ATTACKS_A / NONE
attack_type  = one of: rebuttal / undercutting / undermining / NONE
  rebuttal:     the two conclusions are logically incompatible — they negate the SAME
                proposition, so one claim is true only if the other is false. Merely
                taking the opposing stance, addressing a different sub-topic, or
                "arguing for the other side" is NOT a rebuttal; there must be a direct
                contradiction between the two specific claims. A rebuttal is inherently
                MUTUAL (it holds in both directions); pick either direction.
  undercutting: attacker shows target's conclusion doesn't follow even if target's premise holds
  undermining:  attacker directly attacks a premise the target relies on
  If direction=NONE, attack_type MUST be NONE.
confidence   = integer 0-100: probability that ANY attack exists between the two arguments
  (0 = definitely no attack, 100 = definitely attack)
  If direction=NONE, confidence MUST be 0.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array, same order as the pairs above, no markdown:
[
  {"pair": "id_a:id_b", "direction": "A_ATTACKS_B", "attack_type": "rebuttal", "confidence": 85, "reason": "one sentence"},
  ...
]
raw_response:
[
  {"pair":"pro7:pro63","direction":"B_ATTACKS_A","attack_type":"undermining","confidence":70,"reason":"pro63 claims some abortions are justified, challenging pro7's premise that only health exceptions permit abortion after viability"},
  {"pair":"pro7:pro63b","direction":"B_ATTACKS_A","attack_type":"undermining","confidence":65,"reason":"pro63b argues abortion can be justified on broader grounds, undermining pro7's restriction to health exceptions"},
  {"pair":"pro7:pro58","direction":"NONE","attack_type":"NONE","confidence":20,"reason":"pro58's claim about crime reduction does not directly contradict or undermine pro7's prohibition argument"},
  {"pair":"pro7:pro36","direction":"NONE","attack_type":"NONE","confidence":15,"reason":"pro36 addresses breast‑cancer risk, which is unrelated to pro7's legal‑policy claim"},
  {"pair":"pro7:pro27","direction":"B_ATTACKS_A","attack_type":"undermining","confidence":75,"reason":"pro27 asserts the Constitution does not define a fetus as a person, challenging the legal basis of pro7's protection claim"},
  {"pair":"pro7:con140","direction":"NONE","attack_type":"NONE","confidence":10,"reason":"con140 provides historical opposition to abortion, which aligns rather than attacks pro7"},
  {"pair":"pro7:con23","direction":"NONE","attack_type":"NONE","confidence":10,"reason":"con23 supports ending abortion, consistent with pro7's stance"},
  {"pair":"pro7:con9","direction":"NONE","attack_type":"NONE","confidence":10,"reason":"con9's moral claim against abortion reinforces rather than attacks pro7"},
  {"pair":"pro63:pro58","direction":"NONE","attack_type":"NONE","confidence":15,"reason":"pro63's moral claim and pro58's crime‑reduction claim address different aspects and do not contradict"},
  {"pair":"pro63:pro36","direction":"NONE","attack_type":"NONE","confidence":15,"reason":"pro36 refutes a health‑risk claim, which does not directly oppose pro63's assertion about justification"}
]

parsed.reason: pro63 claims some abortions are justified, challenging pro7's premise that only health exceptions permit abortion after viability
pro63b argues abortion can be justified on broader grounds, undermining pro7's restriction to health exceptions
pro58's claim about crime reduction does not directly contradict or undermine pro7's prohibition argument
pro36 addresses breast‑cancer risk, which is unrelated to pro7's legal‑policy claim
pro27 asserts the Constitution does not define a fetus as a person, challenging the legal basis of pro7's protection claim
con140 provides historical opposition to abortion, which aligns rather than attacks pro7
con23 supports ending abortion, consistent with pro7's stance
con9's moral claim against abortion reinforces rather than attacks pro7
pro63's moral claim and pro58's crime‑reduction claim address different aspects and do not contradict
pro36 refutes a health‑risk claim, which does not directly oppose pro63's assertion about justification

prompt:
Two arguments from a debate on "Abortion":

[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, it may prohibit abortion during that period, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
[pro63b] (pro) Abortion would be justified if the same justification could be applied to killing an adult human.

A previous analysis claims: [pro63b] attacks [pro7]

Does this attack relation genuinely hold?
Does A undermine a premise B relies on (undermining)?
Do A and B assert logically incompatible conclusions that negate the same proposition,
so that one is true only if the other is false (rebuttal)? Opposing stance alone is NOT enough.
Does A show B's conclusion doesn't follow even if B's premise is true (undercutting)?
If any of these holds, confirm=true.

Respond ONLY with JSON, no markdown:
{"confirms": true, "reason": "one sentence"}
raw_response: {"confirms": true, "reason": "pro63b challenges the moral premise that protecting fetal life after viability justifies prohibition, thereby undermining a key premise of pro7"}

parsed.reason: pro63b challenges the moral premise that protecting fetal life after viability justifies prohibition, thereby undermining a key premise of pro7
